title: IEC Code Registration

hero.headline: Get Your Import Export Code with Professionals Law
hero.subtext: Trusted by hundreds of businesses to go global. Fast, affordable & compliant.

mainHeading: IEC Code Registration Online – Apply Import Export License in India

introduction.heading: Introduction and its Compliance
body: In today’s globally connected economy, businesses seeking to expand their operations across the borders require legal authorization in order to trade globally. The Import Export Code (IEC) is a mandatory registration issued by the Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT), Ministry of Commerce, Government of India. Without a valid IEC, no individual or business entity can engage in the import or export of goods and services. At Professionals Law, we offer end-to-end hassle free IEC registration to enable business in initiating and developing their international trade business and to make sure they adhere to the laws.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: The Import Export Code is essential for any business intending to engage in cross-border trade activities. Without IEC, businesses cannot legally import or export goods and services. Without IEC, companies cannot import and export goods and services. Customs, bank and many other regulatory bodies ask for IEC while clearing the shipments from foreign countries and in case of sending money abroad. Professionals Law helps businesses obtain this crucial licence, allowing them to unlock global opportunities and operate with credibility in the international market.

categories.applicantType: Any of the following entities can apply for IEC registration through Professionals Law:
criteria: Individuals intending to engage in import-export activities

categories.applicantType: There is no minimum turnover requirement to obtain IEC.
criteria: Even newly established businesses can apply.

body.title: Legal Authorization
body.description: IEC provides legal sanction to businesses for engaging in import-export activities, ensuring that their transactions comply with international trade regulations.

body.title: Business Expansion
body.description: With IEC, businesses can explore new global markets, expand their reach and increase revenue streams. Professionals Law assists businesses in leveraging this opportunity effectively.

body.title: Customs Clearance and Remittances
body.description: IEC is required for customs clearance of goods and receiving foreign currency payments directly into business accounts.

body.title: Lifetime Validity
body.description: Once issued, the IEC remains valid for the lifetime of the entity, eliminating the need for periodic renewals, unless surrendered or cancelled.

body.title: Simplified Process
body.description: With the expert assistance of Professionals Law, businesses can complete the entire registration process quickly and accurately, avoiding unnecessary delays.

documents: KYC Documents of Directors/partners/owners (Email id, Mobile No. PAN, Aadhaar, Voter id, Bank Statement etc.)
Address proof of the business (Electricity Bill, Rent Agreement, Property Documents)
Cancelled Cheque of the entity’s current account
Incorporation documents (for companies, LLPs and other entities)
Board Resolution or authorization letter (for companies)

body.description: Our experts understand your business requirements and collect all necessary documents.
Professionals Law prepares the online IEC application in the prescribed format as per DGFT guidelines.
The application, along with supporting documents, is submitted electronically to the DGFT portal.
The statutory government fees are paid online.
DGFT reviews the application and, upon verification, issues the IEC in electronic form.
Professionals Law shares the IEC certificate with the client and assists in any post-registration formalities.

body.title: Thus, in most cases, clients receive their IEC certificate
body.description: within 5 to 7 working days, depending on document readiness and DGFT processing time.

body.title: Seizure or detention of goods by customs authorities
Monetary penalties imposed by DGFT and customs
Delays in customs clearance and financial transactions
Legal consequences for non-compliance with the Foreign Trade Act

body.question: Is IEC registration mandatory for export of services?
body.answer: Yes. Even for export of services where foreign remittances are received, IEC is mandatory.

body.question: Do I need to renew my IEC every year?
body.answer: No. IEC is valid for a lifetime unless it is cancelled or surrendered.

body.question: Can an individual apply for IEC without having a business entity?
body.answer: Yes. Individuals can apply for IEC using their personal PAN if they intend to engage in import/export activities.

body.question: What is the government fee for IEC registration?
body.answer: As of now, the government fee is ₹500. Professionals Law offers complete services including professional handling at affordable fees.

body.question: Can IEC be modified after issuance?
body.answer: Yes. Any change in business details can be updated by filing an amendment application on the DGFT portal.

body.question: What happens if IEC is not updated after changes in business details?
body.answer: Failure to update IEC may lead to discrepancies during customs clearance or international transactions.

body.question: Is IEC required for importing goods for personal use?
body.answer: IEC is not required for goods imported strictly for personal use and not for commercial purposes.

body.question: How does Professionals Law help in IEC registration?
body.answer: Professionals Law offers end-to-end assistance including consultation, documentation, application filing and post-registration support ensuring a hassle-free process.

body.question: Can IEC be suspended or cancelled?
body.answer: Yes. DGFT can suspend or cancel IEC in case of violation of regulations. Professionals Law assists in compliance to avoid such situations.
